description: ui-shot/ui-check screenshot-harness coverage + freshness register (tempdoc 615 §6.1a). The harness is verification infrastructure; like any product register it must not silently drift. FRESHNESS: every source path the step index (scripts/jseval/jseval/ui_step_index.json) maps MUST resolve on disk — a deleted/renamed file (the retired-React-stack class that left the harness pointed at modules/ui-web/src/components/**/*.tsx) becomes a build failure, not a silent green. COVERAGE: every RAIL core surface in CORE_SURFACES MUST have a covering view step here or a declared exemption (positive coverage projected from the authority's catalog, same shape as check-declared-surfaces / check-a11y-closure).
fixtureCoverageNote: FIXTURE COVERAGE (tempdoc 615 §37.1): every non-fail-open parseWireContract endpoint in a CAPTURED view surface (viewCoverage[].source) MUST have a deterministic fixtures decision — either a mapped route in ui_fixtures._ROUTES or a fixtureExempt[] row with a reason. An unmapped, non-exempt strict endpoint returns `{}` under --fixtures, trips parseWireContract, and reads as an `app` console error (a fixtures gap masquerading as a real bug — §33). This clause makes the fixture<->contract correspondence asserted, not assumed (same register-guard-resolution shape as the freshness/coverage clauses). SCOPE: the view layer where §33's drift occurred; the api/* layer has its own 564 contract tests and §33 showed it does not trip on initial --fixtures render.

fixtureExempt.reason: SSE stream — install_fixtures serves an empty text/event-stream via its `/stream` special-case, not a JSON fixture, so it never trips the non-fail-open JSON parse on an initial capture (615 §33/§37.1).

exempt.reason: Interaction surface is exercised via the chat-mode + qa-response chain steps, not a standalone nav-view screenshot.
